column1: > **STATUS**: Integrated. `Manuscript_Location` remapped to the manuscript v8 section numbering (wave 3
column2: 2026-07-21); the v7-era citations in rows C13–C17 were corrected at the same time. Machine-readable companion of the Appendix A claim-evidence map.

column3: Claim
column9: Limitations_Caveats

column3: The Phase-1 ceiling is structural under the tested listing/window/local-rewrite model: Phase-1 optimizers achieve ~0% gate reduction when no listing-adjacent inverse/mergeable pairs are exposed.
column9: Only 3/15 families (QFT/GHZ/SurfaceCode) show genuine structural ceiling confirmed by all compilers. 7/15 show prototype action-space ceiling where production peephole optimizers achieve 5-63%. Trichotomy is empirical observation on 15 pre-selected families; not universal law. WCL validation limited to random Universal.

column3: Phase-2a commutation rewriting provides context-dependent improvement on select circuit families.
column9: Improvement is family-dependent. Phase-2a empirical results do not prove Phase-2b template-assisted theorems.

column3: Production compiler gap analysis reveals mechanisms beyond the prototype peephole model.
column9: Hardware-specific effects not fully modeled. Cirq/t|ket> comparisons are present in E20 but require independent verification.

column3: Phase-2a window size w affects the trade-off between optimization power and runtime.
column9: Underpowered comparison (reported power=0.126); treat saturation as empirical trend not confirmed design rule.

column3: Hardware topology constraints affect optimization opportunities differently.
column9: Simplified topology models. Real hardware has additional noise/error effects not modeled.

column3: Clifford+T gate-set decomposition preserves some Phase-2 value among surviving rows.
column9: Survivorship-biased: decomposition/fidelity failures are excluded. Do not generalize to failed rows or full attempted population.

column3: Exact functionality is preserved where fidelity is computed and retained.
column9: Exact/scalable unitary comparison for supported qubit sizes. Failed/invalid rows are filtered where valid fidelity is required.

column3: The Phase-1 reduction ceiling is formally established for bounded circuits with the bounded INSERTION cascade gap resolved.
column9: The unbounded INSERTION cascade remains open. QMA-hardness of CODP remains separate theoretical motivation.

column3: Listing-model dependency: WCL-style preprocessing exposes Phase-1 opportunities hidden under LBL.
column9: Cross-family WCL validation (beyond random Universal) remains future work; Phase-2 commutation opportunities under WCL not separately measured.

column3: Ceiling-aware optimization can skip futile optimization passes with identical reduction and significant speedup.
column9: Exact fidelity verified only for n<=6; larger circuits use target=None (correctness is covered by unit tests).

column3: Multi-compiler extension with Cirq and t|ket> produces comparable reduction and runtime data.
column9: Baseline compiler settings are standardized but may not reflect optimal per-family tuning.

column3: Phase-2b template-assisted BV advantage is theoretically proved and the required template is implemented and unit-tested.
column9: Do not conflate Phase-2b theorem/tests with E10/E11/E14/E16 Phase-2a canonical results. Full canonical-scale Phase-2b benchmark rerun remains future work.

column3: Clifford circuits in Aaronson-Gottesman canonical form have empty Phase-1 action space (R1=0).
column9: Validation uses random AG canonical circuits; does not cover all possible canonical forms exhaustively.

column3: Explicit artificial circuit family with R1=0 and R_{1+2a} >= 1/6.
column9: Construction uses even n>=4; odd n and minimal n=2 not tested.

column3: Phase-2b template matching validated at full scale: BV reaches 69.2% mean reduction hitting the exact k+2 optimum on all 80/80 instances (exceeding the rigorous bound by 3.1-4.2x per instance); IQP 92.0%; Structured 42.3%; RandomClifford 48.2%; pooled Phase-2b reduction 46.5% (95% CI [42.4; 50.5]).
column9: Stratified grid is not a full factorial design; no parity-gadget phase-polynomial templates (QAOA/VQE/HardwareEfficient remain 0%); dataset added to the release manifest as E26_phase2b_full_v8 (wave 3).

column3: Repaired ceiling model (mechanism-gate + random-forest hybrid) achieves LOFO MAE 0.0172 (r=0.977; Spearman rho=0.853; R^2=0.954)
column4: beating the predict-0 baseline MAE of 0.0963.
column9: data/v6/ceiling_repair/
column10: CNOT fold MAE 0.000 (closed by a mechanism gate selected post hoc on a single dataset); family-mean target n=15 underpowered (r=0.059); 11/15 folds have undefined Pearson r; auxiliary classifier F1=0.075; remains exploratory.

column3: Noise-model hardware validation: BV 46.15% logical reduction collapses to 0% after L1 physical transpilation; random instances show small positive Hellinger drift (+0.0016 to +0.0033) in all 4 unitary-verified cases.
column9: Noise-model simulation on fake backends - NOT real hardware. Only 2 retired-device snapshots; small n; do not extrapolate.

column3: Gate-shuffle ablation controls for gate-order effects: counterintuitively shuffled circuits yield higher greedy Phase-1 reduction than original (10.34% vs 6.30%; MWU p=6.8e-15); Phase-2a difference not significant (p=0.462).
column9: Shuffle preserves the gate multiset but not circuit semantics; interpretation limited to listing-model sensitivity.

column3: Multi-seed replication fails to reproduce E04 single-seed point estimates: RLS 0.00% to -176.5%; SA -1.6% to -22.1%; GA -0.2% to -8.3%; only hybrid stays positive at +4.69% (95% CI [4.23; 5.16]).
column9: E29 uses a different circuit configuration (n=5; depth=15) than E04; E04 conclusions are seed/config fragile and must be qualified.
